summary: Russian oil production has dropped by 300,000–400,000 barrels per day in April due to Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries, marking the steepest monthly decline in six years. Belarusian FPV crews from unit 1514, operating under HUR’s “Timur” special unit, have struck Russian targets on the Zaporizhzhia axis. On April 21, 2026, Rubikon Center operators destroyed 234 targets, including Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and personnel, setting a new daily record. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to destroy a Russian TOS-1A MLRS after it was initially damaged by a Bulava loitering munition, and also hit two Russian soldiers assaulting positions on motorcycles. Ukrainian soldiers survived a Russian FPV drone attack in the Kostiantynivka direction, with footage showing a drone detector being used at 0:11 to warn of targeting.
